From: "Security Awareness Team" To: "Cécile Marion" Subject: Login Security Notice: Simulated Risk of Compromised Access Date: Sat, 19 Apr 2087 15:27:00 +0200 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Security Awareness Notice

Security Awareness — Account Takeover (ATO) Simulation

Hello Cécile,

Training Notice: This message is part of an internal security awareness exercise to help you recognize account-takeover (ATO) attempts. This is NOT a real compromise of your account. Please read the guidance below carefully.

As a Business Manager in Mulhouse, you may receive sophisticated messages attempting to trick you into revealing credentials or approving fraudulent access. This simulated email is designed to show common red flags and steps you can take to protect your accounts.

Red flags in suspicious messages

  • Unexpected requests to verify login, password, or payment details.
  • Links that use misspelled or unfamiliar domains instead of official company sites.
  • Urgent language pressuring immediate action (e.g., "access will be suspended").
  • Unusual sender addresses that resemble legitimate ones but have slight differences.

Immediate actions to take

  1. Do not click links or download attachments from suspicious emails.
  2. Verify the sender by checking the full email address and domain.
  3. Use official channels or known bookmarks to sign in and check account status.
  4. Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all business accounts.

Helpful resources:

How to recognize phishing (FTC) CISA Security Alerts

If you think an account may be at risk

Follow these steps right away:

  • Change your password using the account provider's official site.
  • Review recent sign-in activity and connected devices.
  • Notify IT or security support and submit the suspicious message for analysis.

Report this simulated phish: forward the original message (as an attachment if possible) to security@company.com.

Questions? Contact the Security Awareness Team at security-awareness@company.com or call +33 3 00 00 00 00 during business hours.

This is a training communication from the Security Awareness Team. It is intended to help employees recognize and prevent account-takeover attacks. For official account actions, always sign in at the service's legitimate website (do not follow links in suspicious messages).
